title: CODEOWNERS File Modification Monitoring
spl:
index=devops sourcetype="github:pull_request" action IN ("opened","synchronize")
| where mvjoin(commit_files{},",") LIKE "%CODEOWNERS%"
| table _time, repository, author, title, commit_files
description: Attackers may weaken review requirements by editing `CODEOWNERS` or `.github/CODEOWNERS`.
value: Attackers may weaken review requirements by editing `CODEOWNERS` or `.github/CODEOWNERS`.
implementation: Parse file lists from PR payloads. Require CODEOWNER approval for CODEOWNERS changes via branch rules; alert on any edit pending rule rollout.
visualization: Table (PRs touching CODEOWNERS), Timeline, Single value (changes per quarter).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: GitHub pull_request webhook.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: PR `files[]` paths.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Parse file lists from PR payloads. Require CODEOWNER approval for CODEOWNERS changes via branch rules; alert on any edit pending rule rollout.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=devops sourcetype="github:pull_request" action IN ("opened","synchronize")
| where mvjoin(commit_files{},",") LIKE "%CODEOWNERS%"
| table _time, repository, author, title, commit_files
```

Understanding this SPL

**CODEOWNERS File Modification Monitoring** — Attackers may weaken review requirements by editing `CODEOWNERS` or `.github/CODEOWNERS`.

Documented **Data sources**: PR `files[]` paths. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): GitHub pull_request webhook. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: devops; **sourcetype**: github:pull_request. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=devops, sourcetype="github:pull_request". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where mvjoin(commit_files{},",") LIKE "%CODEOWNERS%"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **CODEOWNERS File Modification Monitoring**): table _time, repository, author, title, commit_files


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GitHub audit log. Verify field extractions and index permissions.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (PRs touching CODEOWNERS), Timeline, Single value (changes per quarter).
grandmaExplanation: We watch this part of your delivery chain for risks and reliability so issues show up before they hit customers.
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Change.All_Changes
  by All_Changes.user All_Changes.object All_Changes.action span=1h
| sort -count
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration on Change.All_Changes before using this tstats search.
knownFalsePositives: Activity shifts during holidays, release freezes, or vendor maintenance; confirm against your change calendar.
